signals.headline: US and Iran Planning Second Round of Ceasefire Talks | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US and Iran Planning Second Round of Ceasefire Talks | YES | HIGH confidence**

The **US and Iran are organizing fresh negotiations** before the two-week ceasefire (announced April 7) expires next week.[1] Pakistan has proposed hosting another round of direct talks in the coming days, potentially as early as the end of this week.[4] This represents a confirmed diplomatic development after weekend

signals.headline: Parallel escalation complicates diplomacy**: The Trump administration has implemented a military blockade of Iranian-lin
signals.raw:
Parallel escalation complicates diplomacy**: The Trump administration has implemented a military blockade of Iranian-linked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, which China has condemned as "dangerous and irresponsible."[4] Despite this, the ceasefire is currently holding, with no attacks on US Navy vessels reported, though Iranian drones have targeted Gulf allies in Bahrain.[5]

signals.raw:
Iran Ceasefire Announcement Drives Crypto Rally | YES | HIGH confidence**

Following **ceasefire-related developments, broader markets gained around 5%, while Bitcoin climbed alongside a strong rebound in altcoins, including Ethereum**.[1] Notably, Ethereum surged over 50% in a single week during early April after the Iran ceasefire announcement, while Bitcoin gained roughly 8% over the same windo

signals.headline: Key sticking points remain unresolved**: The US demands removal of Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile and a 20-yea
signals.raw: Key sticking points remain unresolved**: The US demands removal of Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile and a 20-year pause on enrichment, while Iran is pushing back on both points and proposes only a five-year suspension instead.[4] Additionally, Iran insists on greater control over how the Strait of Hormuz is used, rejecting a return to previous arrangements.[1]
